dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 7 responding for unconscious male found on the kitchen floor.
Hi, I'm Jake, an EMT with the rescue squad. Can you tell me what happened here today?
He was here just a minute ago-had a sudden fall and is not responding.
Does he have any known medical issues I should know about?
Not sure-his wife says he has had chemo and is on blood pressure meds.
Ma'am, can you tell me what your name is and where we are?
His eyes are open but sluggish-pupils are 2mm and non-reactive.
No verbal response-AVPU at unresponsive level.
No motor response detected-GCS is at 3.
He's supine, unresponsive with agonal respirations. Can you help me clear the airway?
Airway is partially obstructed-tongue is blocking it.
Let's bring him to the stretcher and use a head tilt/chin lift for visualization.
Agonal-gasping breaths-clear evidence of partial obstruction.
Respiration depth is shallow and irregular-agaloid pattern.
Shallow respirations at 24 per minute-agonal breathing confirmed.
Chest wall is stable with BVM support-bilaterally equal breath sounds.

dialogue.micro_intent: Oxygen saturation measured with pulse oximetry
dialogue.utterance: SpO2 is 58%-needs immediate oxygen intervention.
Carotid pulse is present but weak, around 48 BPM-radial pulse not palpable.
Blood pressure is undetectable-no pulse on manual and digital. 20. Primary Assessment; Skin appearance: Color; Paramedic: Skin is pale, dry, and cool-poor circulation indicators.
No peripheral pulses at this time-no capillary refill.
Capillary refill is delayed, taking over 4 seconds as noted.
Pulse is 48-thready and irregular in rhythm.
Respirations at 24 per minute-shallow and agonal.
SpO2 is 58-confirmed low.
Blood glucose is 117-normal but not assisting with perfusion issues.
ECG shows irregular-initiating 4 lead monitoring. 28. Secondary Assessment; skin exam - Color: Pallor; EMT: Skin is pale but not jaundiced or hyperemic.
Abnormal skin turgor-told you it was dry.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Pupillary reflex / Size of pupils
dialogue.utterance: Pupils are 2mm, equal, and non-reactive.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Neck veins
dialogue.utterance: No JVD-neck veins flat as seen.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - Nuchal rigidity
dialogue.utterance: No nuchal rigidity-no signs of meningeal irritation.

dialogue.micro_intent: head and neck exam - DCAPBTLS noted
dialogue.utterance: Head: no lacerations or deformities-nothing to report.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam - Hemorrhage; Paramedic
dialogue.utterance: No bleeding at ears, nose, or mouth to observe.

dialogue.role: nose, mouth and throat exam - Foreign body; EMT
dialogue.utterance: Oral airway free-no obstruction from foreign bodies.
Chest free of scars to hinder normal rise and fall.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - DCAPBTLS noted
dialogue.utterance: Arms: No deformity, contusions, or abrasions-DCAPBTLS absent.

dialogue.micro_intent: extremities and back exam - DCAPBTLS noted
dialogue.utterance: Legs: No swellings or deformities-no DCAPBTLS found.
What is his medical history as far as you know?
He has a history of stroke, hypertension, and non-insulin diabetes.
He also has ESRD and a history of esophageal cancer.
What was the last thing he ate or drank?

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Just a light lunch-said he felt fine minutes before falling.
Felt lightheaded or dizzy before collapse?
No, just collapsed suddenly-no warning signs.
No spontaneous movement, flatline pain score.
Engaging cardiac arrest protocol-unresponsive, agonal, no circulation.
Kneel, start full CPR-manual compressions initiated.
Respirations at 16-reflect slight improvement.
No detectable pulse-consistent with circulatory arrest.
SpO2 is currently at 0-continues agonal breathing.
Glucose dropped out-0 mg/dL documented.
PEA confirmed-no electrical or mechanical activity.
PEA-manual compressions ongoing.
NPA inserted-ventilation via BVM established.
Bag-valve mask operation confirmed-working well.
IO established-administering 1mg of epinephrine.
Rhythm: PEA-pulse still absent.
AutoPulse compressions engaged-manual switch.
PEA rhythm changing to junctional-heartbeat process.
Confirm patency with capnography-note waveform now.
7.5 mm endotrachial tube placed-good breath compliance.
Reconfiguring BVM to work via ETT-ventilation commenced.
Bolus administered-third dose in progress.
Rechecking BVM-ventilation is stable.
Carotid pulse is back-heartbeat stable at 75 BPM.
Hospital, this is Unit 7-ROSC and junctional rhythm en route.
Patient secured-strapped in safely for transport.
Fourth adenosine bolus administered-epinephrine in progress.
Transporting to [CITY] for critical handling-permits ROSC.
Trauma bed 1-hospital team is on responsibilities.
All IV lines and devices intact-ready for travel.
Pulse remains stable-75 BPM junctional rhythm.
Pre-arrival report directed to staff-recognition en route.
All vents and meds functional-transit safe for critical passenger.
